seoTitle: How to Add a Checkbox in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Build interactive checklists in Microsoft Loop using the plus icon menu or a fast keyboard shortcut, then track task status in real time.
intro: Tracking action items inside a wall of paragraph text makes it easy to lose track of what's actually done. This guide covers two ways to add checkboxes in Microsoft Loop — through the visual component picker and with a quick keyboard shortcut — so you can build a clear, trackable to-do list.

steps.heading: Navigate to your target section
steps.body: Open your Loop page canvas and go to the section where you want the task tracking list.

steps.heading: Use the visual picker
steps.body: Hover your cursor over an empty canvas line, click the plus icon that appears, and select Checklist from the menu.

steps.heading: Or use the keyboard shortcut
steps.body: Type [ ] followed by the spacebar on an empty line to instantly generate an unchecked checkbox item.

steps.heading: Build out your task array
steps.body: Type your task label, press Enter to create the next checkbox line, and repeat until your full list of action items is built.

steps.heading: Mark items complete
steps.body: Click any box to mark it complete and watch the status sync in real time across shared Teams or Outlook channels.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to add a checkbox in Loop?
faq.answer: Typing [ ] followed by the spacebar on an empty line is the quickest way to generate a checkbox without opening a menu.

faq.question: Do checked items sync for everyone viewing the page?
faq.answer: Yes, Loop's collaborative engine keeps checklist status synced in real time across all shared team channels.

faq.question: Can I mix checklists with other content on the same page?
faq.answer: Yes, checklists can sit alongside other text blocks and components to build a highly readable team hub.

faq.question: Can I remove a checklist item once it's added?
faq.answer: Yes, you can delete individual lines from your checklist the same way you would remove any other line of text on the page.
